reason: The node says M_{n_j}(C) acts 'by permuting the n_j copies of sigma_j'. This is verbally imprecise: general elements of M_{n_j}(C) take arbitrary COMPLEX LINEAR COMBINATIONS of copies, not merely permutations. Permutation matrices are a specific (finite) subset of M_{n_j}(C). The formula that follows, (T*(v_1,...,v_{n_j}))_p = sum_q t_{pq} v_q, is correct and describes arbitrary linear combinations, so the formula contradicts the verbal description. Suggest replacing 'by permuting' with 'by taking linear combinations of' or 'by acting on the multiplicity space of'.
